title: US planes at Wick airport during operation to seize tanker

sections.heading: What happened
sections.content: Three  U-28A Dracos  - used by the US Air Force special operations command - stopped at Scottish -government owned Wick airport before heading north.

sections.heading: Key details
sections.content: US planes land at Scottish airport during operation to seize tanker Military aircraft landed at a small civilian airport in the Highlands before flying north during a US Coast Guard operation to board a Russia-bound tanker in the North Atlantic.

sections.heading: Why it matters
sections.content: The Marinera has been accused by the US of breaking its sanctions on the shipping of Iranian oil, and historically the vessel has transported Venezuelan crude oil. BBC Scotland News understands at least three US Air Force planes - U-28A Dracos - took off from RAF Mildenhall in Suffolk and landed at Wick John O'Groats Airport at about 11:00 before leaving at about midday.

sourceReferences.citationText: serper-scottish-aviation – US planes land at Scottish airport during operation to seize tanker
